question: What is the difference between REDO and UNDO?
options: Redo records how to RE-APPLY changes (for recovery); undo records how to REVERSE changes (for rollback and read consistency)
Undo is for recovery, redo is for rollback

explanation.uz: Redo — o'zgarishlarni qayta qo'llash uchun (crash/media recovery). Undo — o'zgarishlarni bekor qilish uchun (ROLLBACK) va boshqa sessiyalarga o'qish izchilligi (eski versiya) uchun. Ular ikki xil maqsad.
explanation.en: Redo records how to re-apply changes (for crash/media recovery). Undo records how to reverse changes (for ROLLBACK) and to give other sessions read consistency (old versions). They serve two different purposes.

question: What are the two main uses of UNDO data?
options: Rolling back transactions, and providing read consistency (serving old row versions to other sessions)

explanation.uz: Undo ikki narsaga kerak: (1) ROLLBACK — tranzaksiyani bekor qilish; (2) read consistency — COMMIT qilinmagan o'zgarish boshqa sessiyalarga ko'rinmasligi uchun ularga qatorning eski versiyasini berish.
explanation.en: Undo serves two things: (1) ROLLBACK — reversing a transaction; (2) read consistency — giving other sessions the old version of a row so they don't see uncommitted changes.

question: Which parameter controls how long undo is retained (for consistent reads / flashback)?

explanation.uz: UNDO_RETENTION (sekundlarda) undo ma'lumoti qancha vaqt saqlanishini belgilaydi — uzoq so'rovlar va flashback uchun. UNDO_TABLESPACE esa qaysi tablespace'da saqlanishini ko'rsatadi.
explanation.en: UNDO_RETENTION (in seconds) controls how long undo is kept — for long-running queries and flashback. UNDO_TABLESPACE names which tablespace stores it.

question: What does the LGWR (Log Writer) background process do?
options: Writes data blocks to the datafiles
Writes the redo log buffer to the online redo log files

explanation.uz: LGWR redo log buffer'dagi yozuvlarni online redo log fayllariga yozadi — masalan COMMIT paytida. Bu tranzaksiya bardoshliligini (durability) ta'minlaydi. (Data bloklarni esa DBWn yozadi.)
explanation.en: LGWR writes the redo entries from the redo log buffer to the online redo log files — e.g. at COMMIT. This guarantees transaction durability. (DBWn writes data blocks.)

question: What is the key difference between ARCHIVELOG and NOARCHIVELOG mode?
options: In ARCHIVELOG mode filled online redo logs are archived (copied), enabling point-in-time recovery and hot (online) backups; NOARCHIVELOG doesn't archive, so you can only restore to the last cold backup

explanation.uz: ARCHIVELOG rejimida to'lgan online redo loglar arxivlanadi (nusxalanadi) — bu vaqt bo'yicha (point-in-time) tiklash va ishlab turgan (hot) backup imkonini beradi. NOARCHIVELOG'da arxiv yo'q — faqat oxirgi cold backup'gacha tiklash mumkin.
explanation.en: In ARCHIVELOG mode filled online redo logs are archived (copied), enabling point-in-time recovery and hot (online) backups. NOARCHIVELOG doesn't archive, so you can only restore to the last cold backup.

question: Which parameter tells Oracle WHERE to write archived redo logs?

explanation.uz: Arxiv redo loglar LOG_ARCHIVE_DEST_n (yoki Fast Recovery Area uchun DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST) bilan belgilangan joyga yoziladi.
explanation.en: Archived redo logs are written to the location set by LOG_ARCHIVE_DEST_n (or DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST for the Fast Recovery Area).

question: What is Oracle Data Guard (a standby database)?
options: A feature that keeps a synchronized standby copy of the primary database (via redo shipping/apply) for disaster recovery and failover
A memory area in the SGA

explanation.uz: Data Guard — asosiy (primary) bazaning sinxron nusxasini (standby) redo'ni uzatib qo'llash orqali saqlab turadi. Ofat yoki nosozlikda standby'ga failover qilinadi — uzluksizlik va falokatdan tiklanish uchun.
explanation.en: Data Guard maintains a synchronized standby copy of the primary database by shipping and applying redo. On disaster or failure you fail over to the standby — for high availability and disaster recovery.

question: A long-running query fails with 'ORA-01555: snapshot too old'. What is the usual cause and fix?
options: The table is locked — kill the blocker
The undo needed for read consistency was overwritten (undo too small or UNDO_RETENTION too low) — increase the undo tablespace / retention, or tune the query
The redo logs are full — add redo

explanation.uz: ORA-01555 — uzoq so'rovga kerak bo'lgan eski undo boshqa tranzaksiyalar tomonidan qayta yozib yuborilgan. Yechim: undo tablespace / UNDO_RETENTION'ni oshirish yoki so'rovni tezlashtirish (undo'ga bog'liqlik vaqtini qisqartirish).
explanation.en: ORA-01555 means the old undo a long query needed was overwritten by other transactions. Fix: increase the undo tablespace / UNDO_RETENTION, or speed up the query to shorten how long it depends on that undo.
